seoTitle: How to Issue a Partial Refund to an eBay Buyer as a Seller
metaDescription: See how to issue a partial refund on eBay from Seller Hub, adjust the amount manually, and keep the order intact instead of cancelling the sale.
intro: When a buyer receives a slightly damaged item or agrees to keep a product for a reduced price, a partial refund lets you resolve the issue without cancelling the whole order. This guide covers processing a partial refund from eBay's Seller Hub, adjusting the exact amount, and understanding how it affects your seller metrics. It applies across most eBay categories, from electronics to clothing to collectibles.

steps.heading: Open the order in Seller Hub
steps.body: Go to your eBay Seller Hub and find the order that needs a partial refund.

steps.heading: Select the refund option
steps.body: Choose the refund action on the order rather than cancelling it, which keeps the transaction on record.

steps.heading: Adjust the refund amount manually
steps.body: Enter the partial amount you're refunding instead of the full order total, reflecting the agreed compensation.

steps.heading: Communicate with the buyer
steps.body: Let the buyer know the partial refund has been issued and why, which helps avoid disputes and keeps feedback positive.

steps.heading: Confirm and monitor the outcome
steps.body: Submit the refund and check that it processes correctly; if the buyer doesn't accept the offer, you may need to follow up through eBay's resolution tools.

faq.question: Does a partial refund on eBay affect my seller metrics?
faq.answer: Refunds, including partial ones, can factor into your seller performance data, though a smoothly handled partial refund is generally better for metrics than a full cancellation or a dispute.

faq.question: Can a buyer reject a partial refund offer?
faq.answer: Yes, the buyer can decline the offer, in which case you may need to negotiate further or escalate through eBay's resolution center.

faq.question: How long does a partial refund take to reach the buyer?
faq.answer: Processing times follow eBay's standard refund timelines, which usually means the funds appear back on the buyer's payment method within a few business days.

faq.question: Do I need to accept a return to issue a partial refund?
faq.answer: No, a partial refund can be issued without requiring the buyer to return the item, which is one of the main reasons sellers use it for minor issues.
